text: Hæc sunt nomina filiorum Israël qui ingressi sunt in Ægyptum cum Jacob : singuli cum domibus suis introierunt :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ruben, Simeon, Levi, Judas,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Issachar, Zabulon et Benjamin,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dan et Nephthali, Gad et Aser.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Erant igitur omnes animæ eorum qui egressi sunt de femore Jacob, septuaginta : Joseph autem in Ægypto erat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quo mortuo, et universis fratribus ejus, omnique cognatione illa,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: filii Israël creverunt, et quasi germinantes multiplicati sunt : ac roborati nimis, impleverunt terram.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Surrexit interea rex novus super Ægyptum, qui ignorabat Joseph.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait ad populum suum : Ecce, populus filiorum Israël multus, et fortior nobis est.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venite, sapienter opprimamus eum, ne forte multiplicetur : et si ingruerit contra nos bellum, addatur inimicis nostris, expugnatisque nobis egrediatur de terra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Præposuit itaque eis magistros operum, ut affligerent eos oneribus : ædificaveruntque urbes tabernaculorum Pharaoni, Phithom et Ramesses.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quantoque opprimebant eos, tanto magis multiplicabantur, et crescebant :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: oderantque filios Israël Ægyptii, et affligebant illudentes eis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: atque ad amaritudinem perducebant vitam eorum operibus duris luti et lateris, omnique famulatu, quo in terræ operibus premebantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit autem rex Ægypti obstetricibus Hebræorum, quarum una vocabatur Sephora, altera Phua,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: præcipiens eis : Quando obstetricabitis Hebræas, et partus tempus advenerit : si masculus fuerit, interficite eum : si femina, reservate.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Timuerunt autem obstetrices Deum, et non fecerunt juxta præceptum regis Ægypti, sed conservabant mares.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quibus ad se accersitis, rex ait : Quidnam est hoc quod facere voluistis, ut pueros servaretis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quæ responderunt : Non sunt Hebreæ sicut ægyptiæ mulieres : ipsæ enim obstetricandi habent scientiam, et priusquam veniamus ad eas, pariunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Bene ergo fecit Deus obstetricibus : et crevit populus, confortatusque est nimis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et quia timuerunt obstetrices Deum, ædificavit eis domos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Præcepit ergo Pharao omni populo suo, dicens : Quidquid masculini sexus natum fuerit, in flumen projicite : quidquid feminini, reservate.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Egressus est post hæc vir de domo Levi : et accepit uxorem stirpis suæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quæ concepit, et peperit filium : et videns eum elegantem, abscondit tribus mensibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque jam celare non posset, sumpsit fiscellam scirpeam, et linivit eam bitumine ac pice : posuitque intus infantulum, et exposuit eum in carecto ripæ fluminis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: stante procul sorore ejus, et considerante eventum rei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce autem descendebat filia Pharaonis ut lavaretur in flumine : et puellæ ejus gradiebantur per crepidinem alvei. Quæ cum vidisset fiscellam in papyrione, misit unam e famulabus suis : et allatam

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: aperiens, cernensque in ea parvulum vagientem, miserta ejus, ait : De infantibus Hebræorum est hic.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cui soror pueri : Vis, inquit, ut vadam, et vocem tibi mulierem hebræam, quæ nutrire possit infantulum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondit : Vade. Perrexit puella et vocavit matrem suam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ad quam locuta filia Pharaonis : Accipe, ait, puerum istum, et nutri mihi : ego dabo tibi mercedem tuam. Suscepit mulier, et nutrivit puerum : adultumque tradidit filiæ Pharaonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quem illa adoptavit in locum filii, vocavitque nomen ejus Moyses, dicens : Quia de aqua tuli eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In diebus illis postquam creverat Moyses, egressus est ad fratres suos : viditque afflictionem eorum, et virum ægyptium percutientem quemdam de Hebræis fratribus suis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque circumspexisset huc atque illuc, et nullum adesse vidisset, percussum Ægyptium abscondit sabulo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et egressus die altero conspexit duos Hebræos rixantes : dixitque ei qui faciebat injuriam : Quare percutis proximum tuum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui respondit : Quis te constituit principem et judicem super nos ? num occidere me tu vis, sicut heri occidisti Ægyptium ? Timuit Moyses, et ait : Quomodo palam factum est verbum istud ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audivitque Pharao sermonem hunc, et quærebat occidere Moysen : qui fugiens de conspectu ejus, moratus est in terra Madian, et sedit juxta puteum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Erant autem sacerdoti Madian septem filiæ, quæ venerunt ad hauriendam aquam : et impletis canalibus adaquare cupiebant greges patris sui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Supervenere pastores, et ejecerunt eas : surrexitque Moyses, et defensis puellis, adaquavit oves earum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quæ cum revertissent ad Raguel patrem suum, dixit ad eas : Cur velocius venistis solito ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Responderunt : Vir ægyptius liberavit nos de manu pastorum : insuper et hausit aquam nobiscum, potumque dedit ovibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: At ille : Ubi est ? inquit : quare dimisistis hominem ? vocate eum ut comedat panem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Juravit ergo Moyses quod habitaret cum eo. Accepitque Sephoram filiam ejus uxorem :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quæ peperit ei filium, quem vocavit Gersam, dicens : Advena fui in terra aliena. Alterum vero peperit, quem vocavit Eliezer, dicens : Deus enim patris mei adjutor meus eripuit me de manu Pharaonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post multum vero tempore mortuus est rex Ægypti : et ingemiscentes filii Israël, propter opera vociferati sunt : ascenditque clamor eorum ad Deum ab operibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audivit gemitum eorum, ac recordatus est fœderis quod pepigit cum Abraham, Isaac et Jacob.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et respexit Dominus filios Israël et cognovit eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Moyses autem pascebat oves Jethro soceri sui sacerdotis Madian : cumque minasset gregem ad interiora deserti, venit ad montem Dei Horeb.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Apparuitque ei Dominus in flamma ignis de medio rubi : et videbat quod rubus arderet, et non combureretur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit ergo Moyses : Vadam, et videbo visionem hanc magnam, quare non comburatur rubus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cernens autem Dominus quod pergeret ad videndum, vocavit eum de medio rubi, et ait : Moyses, Moyses. Qui respondit : Adsum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: At ille : Ne appropies, inquit, huc : solve calceamentum de pedibus tuis : locus enim, in quo stas, terra sancta est.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait : Ego sum Deus patris tui, Deus Abraham, Deus Isaac et Deus Jacob. Abscondit Moyses faciem suam : non enim audebat aspicere contra Deum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cui ait Dominus : Vidi afflictionem populi mei in Ægypto, et clamorem ejus audivi propter duritiam eorum qui præsunt operibus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et sciens dolorem ejus, descendi ut liberem eum de manibus Ægyptiorum, et educam de terra illa in terram bonam, et spatiosam, in terram quæ fluit lacte et melle, ad loca Chananæi et Hethæi, et Amorrhæi, et Pherezæi, et Hevæi, et Jebusæi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Clamor ergo filiorum Israël venit ad me : vidique afflictionem eorum, qua ab Ægyptiis opprimuntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sed veni, et mittam te ad Pharaonem, ut educas populum meum, filios Israël, de Ægypto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Moyses ad Deum : Quis sum ego ut vadam ad Pharaonem, et educam filios Israël de Ægypto ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui dixit ei : Ego ero tecum : et hoc habebis signum, quod miserim te : cum eduxeris populum meum de Ægypto, immolabis Deo super montem istum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ait Moyses ad Deum : Ecce ego vadam ad filios Israël, et dicam eis : Deus patrum vestrorum misit me ad vos. Si dixerint mihi : Quod est nomen ejus ? quid dicam eis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit Deus ad Moysen : Ego sum qui sum. Ait : Sic dices filiis Israël : Qui est, misit me ad vos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque iterum Deus ad Moysen : Hæc dices filiis Israël : Dominus Deus patrum vestrorum, Deus Abraham, Deus Isaac et Deus Jacob, misit me ad vos : hoc nomen mihi est in æternum, et hoc memoriale meum in generationem et generationem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vade, et congrega seniores Israël, et dices ad eos : Dominus Deus patrum vestrorum apparuit mihi, Deus Abraham, Deus Isaac et Deus Jacob, dicens : Visitans visitavi vos : et vidi omnia quæ acciderunt vobis in Ægypto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixi ut educam vos de afflictione Ægypti in terram Chananæi, et Hethæi, et Amorrhæi, et Pherezæi, et Hevæi, et Jebusæi, ad terram fluentem lacte et melle.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audient vocem tuam : ingredierisque tu, et seniores Israël, ad regem Ægypti, et dices ad eum : Dominus Deus Hebræorum vocavit nos : ibimus viam trium dierum in solitudinem, ut immolemus Domino Deo nostro.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sed ego scio quod non dimittet vos rex Ægypti ut eatis nisi per manum validam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Extendam enim manum meam, et percutiam Ægyptum in cunctis mirabilibus meis, quæ facturus sum in medio eorum : post hæc dimittet vos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Daboque gratiam populo huic coram Ægyptiis : et cum egrediemini, non exibitis vacui :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed postulabit mulier a vicina sua et ab hospita sua, vasa argentea et aurea, ac vestes : ponetisque eas super filios et filias vestras, et spoliabitis Ægyptum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondens Moyses ait : Non credent mihi, neque audient vocem meam, sed dicent : Non apparuit tibi Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit ergo ad eum : Quid est quod tenes in manu tua ? Respondit : Virga.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Dominus : Projice eam in terram. Projecit, et versa est in colubrum, ita ut fugeret Moyses.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Dominus : Extende manum tuam, et apprehende caudam ejus. Extendit, et tenuit, versaque est in virgam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ut credant, inquit, quod apparuerit tibi Dominus Deus patrum suorum, Deus Abraham, Deus Isaac et Deus Jacob.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Dominus rursum : Mitte manum tuam in sinum tuum. Quam cum misisset in sinum, protulit leprosam instar nivis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Retrahe, ait, manum tuam in sinum tuum. Retraxit, et protulit iterum, et erat similis carni reliquæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si non crediderint, inquit, tibi, neque audierint sermonem signi prioris, credent verbo signi sequentis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si nec duobus quidem his signis crediderint, neque audierint vocem tuam : sume aquam fluminis, et effunde eam super aridam, et quidquid hauseris de fluvio, vertetur in sanguinem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ait Moyses : Obsecro, Domine, non sum eloquens ab heri et nudiustertius : et ex quo locutus es ad servum tuum, impeditioris et tardioris linguæ sum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit Dominus ad eum : Quis fecit os hominis ? aut quis fabricatus est mutum et surdum, videntem et cæcum ? nonne ego ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Perge, igitur, et ego ero in ore tuo : doceboque te quid loquaris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: At ille : Obsecro, inquit, Domine, mitte quem missurus es.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Iratus Dominus in Moysen, ait : Aaron frater tuus Levites, scio quod eloquens sit : ecce ipse egreditur in occursum tuum, vidensque te lætabitur corde.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Loquere ad eum, et pone verba mea in ore ejus : et ego ero in ore tuo, et in ore illius, et ostendam vobis quid agere debeatis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ipse loquetur pro te ad populum, et erit os tuum : tu autem eris ei in his quæ ad Deum pertinent.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Virgam quoque hanc sume in manu tua, in qua facturus es signa.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Abiit Moyses, et reversus est ad Jethro socerum suum, dixitque ei : Vadam et revertar ad fratres meos in Ægyptum, ut videam si adhuc vivant. Cui ait Jethro : Vade in pace.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit ergo Dominus ad Moysen in Madian : Vade, et revertere in Ægyptum, mortui sunt enim omnes qui quærebant animam tuam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tulit ergo Moyses uxorem suam, et filios suos, et imposuit eos super asinum : reversusque est in Ægyptum, portans virgam Dei in manu sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque ei Dominus revertenti in Ægyptum : Vide ut omnia ostenta quæ posui in manu tua, facias coram Pharaone : ego indurabo cor ejus, et non dimittet populum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dicesque ad eum : Hæc dicit Dominus : Filius meus primogenitus Israël.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixi tibi : Dimitte filium meum ut serviat mihi ; et noluisti dimittere eum : ecce ego interficiam filium tuum primogenitum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque esset in itinere, in diversorio occurrit ei Dominus, et volebat occidere eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tulit idcirco Sephora acutissimam petram, et circumcidit præputium filii sui, tetigitque pedes ejus, et ait : Sponsus sanguinum tu mihi es.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dimisit eum postquam dixerat : Sponsus sanguinum ob circumcisionem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit autem Dominus ad Aaron : Vade in occursum Moysi in desertum. Qui perrexit obviam ei in montem Dei, et osculatus est eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Narravitque Moyses Aaron omnia verba Domini quibus miserat eum, et signa quæ mandaverat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Veneruntque simul, et congregaverunt cunctos seniores filiorum Israël.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Locutusque est Aaron omnia verba quæ dixerat Dominus ad Moysen : et fecit signa coram populo,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et credidit populus. Audieruntque quod visitasset Dominus filios Israël, et respexisset afflictionem illorum : et proni adoraverunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post hæc ingressi sunt Moyses et Aaron, et dixerunt Pharaoni : Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël : Dimitte populum meum ut sacrificet mihi in deserto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: At ille respondit : Quis est Dominus, ut audiam vocem ejus, et dimittam Israël ? nescio Dominum, et Israël non dimittam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixeruntque : Deus Hebræorum vocavit nos, ut eamus viam trium dierum in solitudinem, et sacrificemus Domino Deo nostro : ne forte accidat nobis pestis aut gladius.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ait ad eos rex Ægypti : Quare Moyses et Aaron sollicitatis populum ab operibus suis ? ite ad onera vestra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Pharao : Multus est populus terræ : videtis quod turba succreverit : quanto magis si dederitis eis requiem ab operibus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Præcepit ergo in die illo præfectis operum et exactoribus populi, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nequaquam ultra dabitis paleas populo ad conficiendos lateres, sicut prius : sed ipsi vadant, et colligant stipulas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et mensuram laterum, quam prius faciebant, imponetis super eos, nec minuetis quidquam : vacant enim, et idcirco vociferantur, dicentes : Eamus, et sacrificemus Deo nostro.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Opprimantur operibus, et expleant ea : ut non acquiescant verbis mendacibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur egressi præfecti operum et exactores ad populum, dixerunt : Sic dicit Pharao : Non do vobis paleas :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ite, et colligite sicubi invenire poteritis, nec minuetur quidquam de opere vestro.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dispersusque est populus per omnem terram Ægypti ad colligendas paleas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Præfecti quoque operum instabant, dicentes : Complete opus vestrum quotidie, ut prius facere solebatis quando dabantur vobis paleæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque introduxerit te Dominus in terram Chananæi, et Hethæi, et Amorrhæi, et Hevæi, et Jebusæi, quam juravit patribus tuis ut daret tibi, terram fluentem lacte et melle, celebrabis hunc morem sacrorum mense isto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et erit quasi signum in manu tua, et quasi monimentum ante oculos tuos : et ut lex Domini semper sit in ore tuo, in manu enim forti eduxit te Dominus de Ægypto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque introduxerit te Dominus in terram Chananæi, sicut juravit tibi et patribus tuis, et dederit tibi eam :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit autem Dominus ad Moysen : Usquequo non vultis custodire mandata mea et legem meam ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: videte quod Dominus dederit vobis sabbatum, et propter hoc die sexta tribuit vobis cibos duplices : maneat unusquisque apud semetipsum ; nullus egrediatur de loco suo die septimo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sabbatizavit populus die septimo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Appellavitque domus Israël nomen ejus Man : quod erat quasi semen coriandri album, gustusque ejus quasi similæ cum melle.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit autem Moyses : Iste est sermo, quem præcepit Dominus : Imple gomor ex eo, et custodiatur in futuras retro generationes : ut noverint panem, quo alui vos in solitudine, quando educti estis de terra Ægypti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Moyses ad Aaron : Sume vas unum, et mitte ibi man, quantum potest capere gomor, et repone coram Domino ad servandum in generationes vestras,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sicut præcepit Dominus Moysi. Posuitque illud Aaron in tabernaculo reservandum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Filii autem Israël comederunt man quadraginta annis, donec venirent in terram habitabilem : hoc cibo aliti sunt, usquequo tangerent fines terræ Chanaan.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Gomor autem decima pars est ephi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur profecta omnis multitudo filiorum Israël de deserto Sin per mansiones suas, juxta sermonem Domini, castrametati sunt in Raphidim, ubi non erat aqua ad bibendum populo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui jurgatus contra Moysen, ait : Da nobis aquam, ut bibamus. Quibus respondit Moyses : Quid jurgamini contra me ? cur tentatis Dominum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sitivit ergo ibi populus præ aquæ penuria, et murmuravit contra Moysen, dicens : Cur fecisti nos exire de Ægypto, ut occideres nos, et liberos nostros, ac jumenta siti ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Clamavit autem Moyses ad Dominum, dicens : Quid faciam populo huic ? adhuc paululum, et lapidabit me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait Dominus ad Moysen : Antecede populum, et sume tecum de senioribus Israël : et virgam qua percussisti fluvium, tolle in manu tua, et vade.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: En ego stabo ibi coram te, supra petram Horeb : percutiesque petram, et exibit ex ea aqua, ut bibat populus. Fecit Moyses ita coram senioribus Israël :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et vocavit nomen loci illius, Tentatio, propter jurgium filiorum Israël, et quia tentaverunt Dominum, dicentes : Estne Dominus in nobis, an non ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venit autem Amalec, et pugnabat contra Israël in Raphidim.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Moyses ad Josue : Elige viros : et egressus, pugna contra Amalec : cras ego stabo in vertice collis, habens virgam Dei in manu mea.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fecit Josue ut locutus erat Moyses, et pugnavit contra Amalec : Moyses autem et Aaron et Hur ascenderunt super verticem collis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque levaret Moyses manus, vincebat Israël : sin autem paululum remisisset, superabat Amalec.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Manus autem Moysi erant graves : sumentes igitur lapidem, posuerunt subter eum, in quo sedit : Aaron autem et Hur sustentabant manus ejus ex utraque parte. Et factum est ut manus illius non lassarentur usque ad occasum solis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fugavitque Josue Amalec, et populum ejus in ore gladii.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit autem Dominus ad Moysen : Scribe hoc ob monimentum in libro, et trade auribus Josue : delebo enim memoriam Amalec sub cælo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ædificavitque Moyses altare : et vocavit nomen ejus, Dominus exaltatio mea, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia manus solii Domini, et bellum Domini erit contra Amalec, a generatione in generationem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque audisset Jethro, sacerdos Madian, cognatus Moysi, omnia quæ fecerat Deus Moysi, et Israëli populo suo, et quod eduxisset Dominus Israël de Ægypto,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: tulit Sephoram uxorem Moysi quam remiserat,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et duos filios ejus : quorum unus vocabatur Gersam, dicente patre : Advena fui in terra aliena ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: alter vero Eliezer : Deus enim, ait, patris mei adjutor meus, et eruit me de gladio Pharaonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venit ergo Jethro cognatus Moysi, et filii ejus, et uxor ejus ad Moysen in desertum, ubi erat castrametatus juxta montem Dei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et mandavit Moysi, dicens : Ego Jethro cognatus tuus venio ad te, et uxor tua, et duo filii cum ea.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui egressus in occursum cognati sui, adoravit, et osculatus est eum : salutaveruntque se mutuo verbis pacificis. Cumque intrasset tabernaculum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: narravit Moyses cognato suo cuncta quæ fecerat Dominus Pharaoni et Ægyptiis propter Israël : universumque laborem, qui accidisset eis in itinere, et quod liberaverat eos Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Lætatusque est Jethro super omnibus bonis, quæ fecerat Dominus Israëli, eo quod eruisset eum de manu Ægyptiorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait : Benedictus Dominus, qui liberavit vos de manu Ægyptiorum, et de manu Pharaonis ; qui eruit populum suum de manu Ægypti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nunc cognovi, quia magnus Dominus super omnes deos : eo quod superbe egerint contra illos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Obtulit ergo Jethro cognatus Moysi holocausta et hostias Deo : veneruntque Aaron et omnes seniores Israël, ut comederent panem cum eo coram Deo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Altera autem die sedit Moyses ut judicaret populum, qui assistebat Moysi a mane usque ad vesperam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod cum vidisset cognatus ejus, omnia scilicet quæ agebat in populo, ait : Quid est hoc quod facis in plebe ? cur solus sedes, et omnis populus præstolatur de mane usque ad vesperam ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cui respondit Moyses : Venit ad me populus quærens sententiam Dei :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cumque acciderit eis aliqua disceptatio, veniunt ad me ut judicem inter eos, et ostendam præcepta Dei, et leges ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: At ille : Non bonam, inquit, rem facis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Stulto labore consumeris et tu, et populus iste qui tecum est : ultra vires tuas est negotium ; solus illud non poteris sustinere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sed audi verba mea atque consilia, et erit Deus tecum. Esto tu populo in his quæ ad Deum pertinent, ut referas quæ dicuntur ad eum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ostendasque populo cæremonias et ritum colendi, viamque per quam ingredi debeant, et opus quod facere debeant.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Provide autem de omni plebe viros potentes, et timentes Deum, in quibus sit veritas, et qui oderint avaritiam, et constitue ex eis tribunos, et centuriones, et quinquagenarios, et decanos,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui judicent populum omni tempore : quidquid autem majus fuerit, referant ad te, et ipsi minora tantummodo judicent : leviusque sit tibi, partito in alios onere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si hoc feceris, implebis imperium Dei, et præcepta ejus poteris sustentare : et omnis hic populus revertetur ad loca sua cum pace.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quibus auditis, Moyses fecit omnia quæ ille suggesserat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et electis viris strenuis de cuncto Israël, constituit eos principes populi, tribunos, et centuriones, et quinquagenarios, et decanos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui judicabant plebem omni tempore : quidquid autem gravius erat, referebant ad eum, faciliora tantummodo judicantes.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dimisitque cognatum suum : qui reversus abiit in terram suam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mense tertio egressionis Israël de terra Ægypti, in die hac venerunt in solitudinem Sinai.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nam profecti de Raphidim, et pervenientes usque in desertum Sinai, castrametati sunt in eodem loco, ibique Israël fixit tentoria e regione montis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Moyses autem ascendit ad Deum : vocavitque eum Dominus de monte, et ait : Hæc dices domui Jacob, et annuntiabis filiis Israël :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vos ipsi vidistis quæ fecerim Ægyptiis, quomodo portaverim vos super alas aquilarum, et assumpserim mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si ergo audieritis vocem meam, et custodieritis pactum meum, eritis mihi in peculium de cunctis populis : mea est enim omnis terra :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et vos eritis mihi in regnum sacerdotale, et gens sancta. Hæc sunt verba quæ loqueris ad filios Israël.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venit Moyses : et convocatis majoribus natu populi, exposuit omnes sermones quos mandaverat Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Responditque omnis populus simul : Cuncta quæ locutus est Dominus, faciemus. Cumque retulisset Moyses verba populi ad Dominum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ait ei Dominus : Jam nunc veniam ad te in caligine nubis, ut audiat me populus loquentem ad te, et credat tibi in perpetuum. Nuntiavit ergo Moyses verba populi ad Dominum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui dixit ei : Vade ad populum, et sanctifica illos hodie, et cras, laventque vestimenta sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sint parati in diem tertium : in die enim tertia descendet Dominus coram omni plebe super montem Sinai.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Constituesque terminos populo per circuitum, et dices ad eos : Cavete ne ascendatis in montem, nec tangatis fines illius : omnis qui tetigerit montem, morte morietur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Manus non tanget eum, sed lapidibus opprimetur, aut confodietur jaculis : sive jumentum fuerit, sive homo, non vivet : cum cœperit clangere buccina, tunc ascendant in montem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Descenditque Moyses de monte ad populum, et sanctificavit eum. Cumque lavissent vestimenta sua,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ait ad eos : Estote parati in diem tertium, et ne appropinquetis uxoribus vestris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Jamque advenerat tertius dies, et mane inclaruerat : et ecce cœperunt audiri tonitrua, ac micare fulgura, et nubes densissima operire montem, clangorque buccinæ vehementius perstrepebat : et timuit populus qui erat in castris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque eduxisset eos Moyses in occursum Dei de loco castrorum, steterunt ad radices montis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Totus autem mons Sinai fumabat, eo quod descendisset Dominus super eum in igne : et ascenderet fumus ex eo quasi de fornace, eratque omnis mons terribilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sonitus buccinæ paulatim crescebat in majus, et prolixius tendebatur : Moyses loquebatur, et Deus respondebat ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Descenditque Dominus super montem Sinai in ipso montis vertice, et vocavit Moysen in cacumen ejus. Quo cum ascendisset,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: dixit ad eum : Descende, et contestare populum : ne forte velit transcendere terminos ad videndum Dominum, et pereat ex eis plurima multitudo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sacerdotes quoque qui accedunt ad Dominum, sanctificentur, ne percutiat eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixitque Moyses ad Dominum : Non poterit vulgus ascendere in montem Sinai : tu enim testificatus es, et jussisti, dicens : Pone terminos circa montem, et sanctifica illum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cui ait Dominus : Vade, descende : ascendesque tu, et Aaron tecum : sacerdotes autem et populus ne transeant terminos, nec ascendant ad Dominum, ne forte interficiat illos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Descenditque Moyses ad populum, et omnia narravit eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Locutusque est Dominus cunctos sermones hos :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ego sum Dominus Deus tuus, qui eduxi te de terra Ægypti, de domo servitutis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non habebis deos alienos coram me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non facies tibi sculptile, neque omnem similitudinem quæ est in cælo desuper, et quæ in terra deorsum, nec eorum quæ sunt in aquis sub terra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non adorabis ea, neque coles : ego sum Dominus Deus tuus fortis, zelotes, visitans iniquitatem patrum in filios, in tertiam et quartam generationem eorum qui oderunt me :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et faciens misericordiam in millia his qui diligunt me, et custodiunt præcepta mea.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non assumes nomen Domini Dei tui in vanum : nec enim habebit insontem Dominus eum qui assumpserit nomen Domini Dei sui frustra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Memento ut diem sabbati sanctifices.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sex diebus operaberis, et facies omnia opera tua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Septimo autem die sabbatum Domini Dei tui est : non facies omne opus in eo, tu, et filius tuus et filia tua, servus tuus et ancilla tua, jumentum tuum, et advena qui est intra portas tuas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sex enim diebus fecit Dominus cælum et terram, et mare, et omnia quæ in eis sunt, et requievit in die septimo : idcirco benedixit Dominus diei sabbati, et sanctificavit eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Honora patrem tuum et matrem tuam, ut sis longævus super terram, quam Dominus Deus tuus dabit tibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non occides.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non mœchaberis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non furtum facies.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non loqueris contra proximum tuum falsum testimonium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non concupisces domum proximi tui, nec desiderabis uxorem ejus, non servum, non ancillam, non bovem, non asinum, nec omnia quæ illius sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cunctus autem populus videbat voces et lampades, et sonitum buccinæ, montemque fumantem : et perterriti ac pavore concussi, steterunt procul,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: dicentes Moysi : Loquere tu nobis, et audiemus : non loquatur nobis Dominus, ne forte moriamur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait Moyses ad populum : Nolite timere : ut enim probaret vos venit Deus, et ut terror illius esset in vobis, et non peccaretis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Stetitque populus de longe. Moyses autem accessit ad caliginem in qua erat Deus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit præterea Dominus ad Moysen : Hæc dices filiis Israël : Vos vidistis quod de cælo locutus sim vobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non facietis deos argenteos, nec deos aureos facietis vobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Altare de terra facietis mihi, et offeretis super eo holocausta et pacifica vestra, oves vestras et boves in omni loco in quo memoria fuerit nominis mei : veniam ad te, et benedicam tibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si altare lapideum feceris mihi, non ædificabis illud de sectis lapidibus : si enim levaveris cultrum super eo, polluetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non ascendes per gradus ad altare meum, ne reveletur turpitudo tua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc sunt judicia quæ propones eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si emeris servum hebræum, sex annis serviet tibi : in septimo egredietur liber gratis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum quali veste intraverit, cum tali exeat : si habens uxorem, et uxor egredietur simul.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sin autem dominus dederit illi uxorem, et pepererit filios et filias : mulier et liberi ejus erunt domini sui, ipse vero exibit cum vestitu suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si dixerit servus : Diligo dominum meum et uxorem ac liberos ; non egrediar liber :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: offeret eum dominus diis, et applicabitur ad ostium et postes, perforabitque aurem ejus subula : et erit ei servus in sæculum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si quis vendiderit filiam suam in famulam, non egredietur sicut ancillæ exire consueverunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si displicuerit oculis domini sui cui tradita fuerat, dimittet eam : populo autem alieno vendendi non habebit potestatem, si spreverit eam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sin autem filio suo desponderit eam, juxta morem filiarum faciet illi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si alteram ei acceperit, providebit puellæ nuptias, et vestimenta, et pretium pudicitiæ non negabit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si tria ista non fecerit, egredietur gratis absque pecunia.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui percusserit hominem volens occidere, morte moriatur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si quis per industriam occiderit proximum suum, et per insidias : ab altari meo evelles eum, ut moriatur.

text: Qui percusserit patrem suum aut matrem, morte moriatur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si egressus ignis invenerit spinas, et comprehenderit acervos frugum, sive stantes segetes in agris, reddet damnum qui ignem succenderit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si furto ablatum fuerit, restituet damnum domino ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si pater virginis dare noluerit, reddet pecuniam juxta modum dotis, quam virgines accipere consueverunt.

text: Maleficos non patieris vivere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Viduæ et pupillo non nocebitis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sex annis seminabis terram tuam, et congregabis fruges ejus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cingulum vero de bysso retorta, hyacintho, purpura, ac vermiculo bis tincto, arte plumaria, sicut præceperat Dominus Moysi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fecerunt et laminam sacræ venerationis de auro purissimo, scripseruntque in ea opere gemmario, Sanctum Domini :

text: et strinxerunt eam cum mitra vitta hyacinthina, sicut præceperat Dominus Moysi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Perfectum est igitur omne opus tabernaculi et tecti testimonii : feceruntque filii Israël cuncta quæ præceperat Dominus Moysi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: opertorium de pellibus arietum rubricatis, et aliud operimentum de janthinis pellibus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: mensam cum vasis suis et propositionis panibus ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: tentorium in introitu atrii, funiculosque illius et paxillos. Nihil ex vasis defuit, quæ in ministerium tabernaculi, et in tectum fœderis jussa sunt fieri.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vestes quoque, quibus sacerdotes utuntur in sanctuario, Aaron scilicet et filii ejus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quæ postquam Moyses cuncta vidit completa, benedixit eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Locutusque est Dominus ad Moysen, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mense primo, prima die mensis, eriges tabernaculum testimonii,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et pones in eo arcam, dimittesque ante illam velum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et illata mensa, pones super eam quæ rite præcepta sunt. Candelabrum stabit cum lucernis suis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et altare aureum, in quo adoletur incensum, coram arca testimonii. Tentorium in introitu tabernaculi pones,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et ante illud altare holocausti :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: labrum inter altare et tabernaculum, quod implebis aqua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Circumdabisque atrium tentoriis, et ingressum ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et assumpto unctionis oleo unges tabernaculum cum vasis suis, ut sanctificentur :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: altare holocausti et omnia vasa ejus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: labrum cum basi sua : omnia unctionis oleo consecrabis, ut sint Sancta sanctorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Applicabisque Aaron et filios ejus ad fores tabernaculi testimonii, et lotos aqua

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: indues sanctis vestibus, ut ministrent mihi, et unctio eorum in sacerdotium sempiternum proficiat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fecitque Moyses omnia quæ præceperat Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur mense primo anni secundi, prima die mensis, collocatum est tabernaculum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Erexitque Moyses illud, et posuit tabulas ac bases et vectes, statuitque columnas,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et expandit tectum super tabernaculum, imposito desuper operimento, sicut Dominus imperaverat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posuit et testimonium in arca, subditis infra vectibus, et oraculum desuper.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque intulisset arcam in tabernaculum, appendit ante eam velum ut expleret Domini jussionem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posuit et mensam in tabernaculo testimonii ad plagam septentrionalem extra velum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posuit et candelabrum in tabernaculo testimonii e regione mensæ in parte australi,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: locatis per ordinem lucernis, juxta præceptum Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posuit et altare aureum sub tecto testimonii contra velum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et adolevit super eo incensum aromatum, sicut jusserat Dominus Moysi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posuit et tentorium in introitu tabernaculi testimonii,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et altare holocausti in vestibulo testimonii, offerens in eo holocaustum, et sacrificia, ut Dominus imperaverat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Labrum quoque statuit inter tabernaculum testimonii et altare, implens illud aqua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Laveruntque Moyses et Aaron ac filii ejus manus suas et pedes,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cum ingrederentur tectum fœderis, et accederent ad altare, sicut præceperat Dominus Moysi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Erexit et atrium per gyrum tabernaculi et altaris, ducto in introitu ejus tentorio. Postquam omnia perfecta sunt,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: operuit nubes tabernaculum testimonii, et gloria Domini implevit illud.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nec poterat Moyses ingredi tectum fœderis, nube operiente omnia, et majestate Domini coruscante, quia cuncta nubes operuerat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Siquando nubes tabernaculum deserebat, proficiscebantur filii Israël per turmas suas :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si pendebat desuper, manebant in eodem loco.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nubes quippe Domini incubabat per diem tabernaculo, et ignis in nocte, videntibus cunctis populis Israël per cunctas mansiones suas.
